title: Lemon Curd
description: My Thermomix take on Ina Garten's lemon curd — glossy, tangy and impossible to scramble.
intro: Lemon curd is one of those things the Thermomix was made for — it stirs and heats gently so the eggs never scramble. Ina's is bright, buttery and just sweet enough.
steps: Add the lemon zest and sugar to the bowl and mill 10 sec / 0°C / speed 9 to a fragrant lemon sugar.
Add the lemon juice, eggs, butter and salt. Cook 8 min / 80°C / speed 4 until thickened and glossy.
Pour into a sterilised jar and cool. It thickens more as it chills — keeps 2 weeks in the fridge; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: The gentle 80°C is why it never scrambles.
Spread it on the Mary Berry lemon drizzle cake, scones or yoghurt.

faq.q: Why won't the curd scramble in a Thermomix?
faq.a: Constant speed-4 stirring at a low 80°C cooks the eggs gently and evenly.

faq.q: Is this the original Ina Garten recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Ina Garten and lemon curd; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
